zone_6b_notes: Zone 6b is the cold edge of lavender's range. English lavender (angustifolia) is the hardiest â€” survives Farmington winters with excellent drainage and a south-facing exposure. Heavy clay Missouri soil is problematic; amend with grit or grow in raised beds. Mulch lightly with gravel, not wood chips (traps moisture). Cut back by one-third in early spring â€” do not cut into old wood.
water: low â€” excellent drainage essential; wet winters kill lavender faster than cold
zone_notes_warning: Marginally hardy in Zone 6b â€” may need winter protection in harsh years. Raised bed planting strongly recommended.
